page: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 9V74 cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16, and SHA extensions. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities for remote disk attachment.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core execution, including PassMark CPU and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls within the average range. However, memory subsystem performance presents a notable tradeoff, with both small-block and large-block read-only memory bandwidth testing in the bottom 25%. Computational tasks such as text processing perform strongly, while software compilation with Clang and ray tracing benchmarks are weak.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D4as_v6 offers stable resource density for general-purpose workloads. It is suited for static web serving, Redis key-value operations, and lightweight single-threaded processing. It is less optimal for memory-bandwidth-intensive applications, database operations, or medium-to-large language model inference, where prompt processing speeds drop significantly.
description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, requiring remote Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and text processing, though multi-core performance and memory write bandwidth remain average. Tradeoffs include weak read-only memory bandwidth and poor performance in software compilation and medium-model LLM inference. This server is suited for static web serving, Redis deployments, and general-purpose applications that do not require high memory throughput or local disk storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong single-core performance with average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC general-purpose virtual machine offering strong single-core performance and balanced memory allocation without local storage.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor
16.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage, utilizing remote Premium Storage configurations
Suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and general-purpose workloads
